stories.title: MAGA’s Romanian champion George Simion eyes comeback as government implodes
stories.summary: Hard-right firebrand George Simion lost Romania’s presidential race last year , but as the country’s centrist government coalition crumbles, he now sees an alternative route to power.   Romania’s mainstream pro-EU parties are scrambling to keep the ultranationalist and euroskeptic Simion on the sidelines while they negotiate potential resolutions to the crisis. But the MAGA-aligned politician is way ahead in national polls, and insists it’s time his party had a shot at gov

stories.title: Trump got her out of a KGB prison. Now she needs him to help free her husband.
stories.summary: Few people experience such a sudden reversal in their lives: from being held captive in a KGB prison by one of the world’s most brutal regimes to freedom in Europe. Journalist Katsiaryna Andreeva is one of them. Arrested in 2020 for filming a protest against Belarusian strongman Alexander Lukashenko from a 14th-floor window, Andreeva was first sentenced to two years in prison on charges of “violating public order.” In July 2022, she was hit with a second charge, treason, and condemned to another

stories.title: The Guardian view on Anthropic’s Claude Mythos: when AI finds every flaw, who controls the internet? | Editorial
stories.summary: Tech can scale cyber-attacks and defences alike, raising questions about private power, public risk and the future of a shared internet Anthropic announced its latest AI model, Claude Mythos , this month but said it would not be released publicly, because it turns computers into crime scenes. The company claimed that it could find previously unknown “zero-day” flaws, exploit them and, in principle, link these weaknesses in order to take over major operating systems and web browsers . Mythos did

stories.title: Ukraine: Russian strike on Odesa kills 2, hits hospitals and schools overnight
stories.summary: Ukraine said Russian forces struck the Black Sea port city of Odesa overnight on 23 to 24 April 2026, killing at least two people and injuring more than a dozen.

stories.title: EU leaders under pressure to break €1.8 trillion budget deadlock — live updates
stories.summary: EU leaders are gathering Friday morning for their second day of talks in Cyprus. Top of the agenda: injecting some political momentum into negotiations on the bloc’s next seven-year budget, worth €1.8 trillion. European Council President António Costa is on a mission to break negotiators’ deadlock and make progress on the discussions, which have got bogged down in technical disputes and brinkmanship on sensitive areas. In the afternoon, leaders will host their Gulf counterparts to talk abou

stories.title: Europe is in a profound state of crisis. Luckily, we know what to do | Nathalie Tocci and Anu Bradford
stories.summary: We assembled a group of the continent’s leading thinkers to assess the threats: their warnings are stark, but the remedy is within reach Caught between Vladimir Putin’s Russia, Donald Trump’s US and Xi Jinping’s China, Europe appears in a state of profound crisis, the narrative about its future often filled with fatalism. There is a paradox, however. Despite rising nationalism, the climate crisis and the economic slowdown , few would take issue with the claim that Europe still has a great deal g

stories.title: At first summit without Orbán, Zelenskyy rejects “symbolic” EU membership
stories.summary: Also in this newsletter: EU approves €90 billion loan for Ukraine after Hungary lifts controversial veto.

stories.title: EU formally approves €90bn Ukraine loan and 20th sanctions package against Russia
stories.summary: Ursula von der Leyen hails ‘good news’ after Hungary’s lifting of vetoes allows leaders to sign off on agreements EU leaders have welcomed the end of diplomatic deadlock over a long-awaited €90bn (£78bn) loan for Ukraine, after the bloc completed the agreement along with a 20th sanctions package against Russia. After weeks of delay, the EU signed off on the loan on Thursday, in time for a summit in Cyprus that began in the evening and will include talks over a dinner with the Ukrainian leader, V

stories.title: Woman killed by bear in Polish forest, son and local government say
stories.summary: In an extremely rare fatal encounter, the 58-year-old was killed as she was walking in a mountainous area, her son told rescuers.

stories.title: Aggasztóan elszaporodtak Magyarországon a szarvasok
stories.summary: Becslések szerint 250 ezer példány flangál Magyarországon, ami rengeteg bajt és kockázatot jelent. Az erdészek és a vadászok a probléma mértékében és kezelési módjában sem értenek egyet.

stories.title: Zelenszkij: Orbánnak nem volt igaza, nem szabad szítani a gyűlöletet a szomszédaink iránt, látjuk az eredményt
stories.summary: Békében kell élnünk – üzente Ukrajna elnöke a magyaroknak az EU-csúcsról, amire ő elment, a magyar kormányfő nem.

stories.title: A Tisza kampányfőnöke: 2022-ben is nyerhetett volna az ellenzék, de Márki-Zay nem kért a tanácsából
stories.summary: Tóth Péter szerint az volt a különbség a 2022-es összellenzék és a 2024-es EP-s Tisza-eredmények között, hogy előbbi két nap alatt égett el, utóbbi fenntartotta és növelte bázisát. Arról is beszélt, hogy szerinte Magyar Péter miniszterelnökként sem hagyhatja abba az országjárást.

stories.title: ByeAlex újra mentor lesz az X-Faktorban
stories.summary: A Solére művésznéven alkotó Szigeti Zsófia is új szereplő lesz a mentorcsapatban.

stories.title: Mit tehet egy jogállam, ha a közpénz elveszti közvagyon jellegét?
stories.summary: Az, hogy a magyar társadalom képes lesz-e a jogállami normákat betartva leszámolni a lopással és a korrupcióval, generációkra meghatározhatja az ország sorsát.

stories.title: A gyermekvonatok, amikkel egy évszázada hatvanezer gyerek utazott Magyarországról Nyugat-Európába
stories.summary: Pontosan száz éve tért haza a századik gyermekvonat Antwerpenből. Az első világháború után traumatizált gyerekeket fogadtak be holland, belga, svájci családok, általában átmenetileg, de olykor végleg.

stories.title: Mi igaz abból, amit az orosz gázról mondott Szijjártó Péter?
stories.summary: A külügyminiszter árnyalta az orosz beszerzések áráról alkotott képet, de így is minden adat azt mutatja, hogy eltúlozta az orosz földgáz szerepét a kedvezményes lakossági árszint fenntartásában.

stories.title: A horvát kormányfő szerint a Barátságot pótló Adria-kőolajvezeték bebizonyította, hogy működőképes
stories.summary: Andrej Plenković nagyon jót beszélgetett Magyar Péterrel, és nagyon jó együttműködésre számít a 27 uniós vezető között.

stories.title: A CEU nem költözik vissza Budapestre
stories.summary: A bécsi campust nem költöztetik vissza.

stories.title: Donald Tusk szerint türelmesnek kell lenni Magyar Péterrel, de ő is okozhat csalódást egyeseknek
stories.summary: A lengyel miniszterelnök szerint hiába várja az EU-s baloldal, hogy Magyar Péter progresszív lesz.

stories.title: Co Lee és a 33 dalos Air 2: új korszak indul a Budapest Parkban (x)
stories.summary: Április 20-án debütál Co Lee és don sea közös nagylemeze, az Air 2, amely összesen 26 trackkel és egy különleges vinyl-kiadáson 7 bónuszdallal feszegeti a hazai hiphop határait. A WAVY Records gondozásában megjelent anyag alkotói folyamata egyfajta belső meditációként, lassan hömpölygő folyóként érkezik a hallgatókhoz. A valaha volt legnagyobb volumenű magyar nyelvű lo-fi hiphop anyag négyévnyi munka gyümölcseként ölt testet.

stories.title: Prosecutor’s office suddenly eager to investigate suspicious cases in Hungary, which speaks volumes about fallen regime
stories.summary: Immediately after Fidesz's crushing defeat, the prosecutor’s office took action in two cases in which virtually nothing had happened up to that point. A look at why this may be, and what could still be expected in light of the Tisza Party's promise of calling to account.

stories.title: Márciusban visszafordult az ijesztő trend a magyar munkanélküliségben
stories.summary: Egy hirtelen megugrás után ismét mérséklődött az adat, de azért még mindig nem rózsásak a várakozások a munkaerőpiacon.

stories.title: Fico helyettese szerint a Beneš-dekrétumokkal a szlovák állam csak visszaveszi, ami az övé
stories.summary: A parlamentben is téma volt a földek kérdése, amibe a szlovák kormány most a korábbiaknál jobban beleáll.

stories.title: Hungarians have been using these lands illegally since 1945 – Slovak deputy PM says
stories.summary: Robert Kaliňák spoke about one of the most sensitive issues affecting future Hungarian-Slovak relations: the Beneš Decrees.

stories.title: Newsletter: EU leaders weigh a NATO-style common defence clause
stories.summary: Also in this newsletter: EU unblocks €90bn Ukraine loan and new Russia sanctions.

stories.title: Egykori MNB-alelnök: Simor Andrásnak az Erstében betöltött tisztségével összefüggő döntésekre nekem nincs és nem is volt ráhatásom
stories.summary: A Magyar Nemzeti Bank közölte, hogy vizsgálatot indít a kérdéses időszakra vonatkozóan.

stories.title: Bányafelügyeleti eljárás indult Orbán Viktor testvérének és anyjának közös cége ellen
stories.summary: A kitermelési mennyiségek miatt indul vizsgálat a Hahót Tőzeg Kft.-vel szemben.

stories.title: Átlépte az egymillió megtekintést a Szijjártó Péterrel készített interjúnk
stories.summary: A leköszönő külgazdasági és külügyminisztert élőben, több mint három órán keresztül kérdezte Fábián Tamás.

stories.title: It took a massive propaganda campaign to turn Hungarians into potato lovers
stories.summary: Potatoes are such an important part of Hungarian cuisine that it is hard to imagine that as recently as two hundred years ago, people did not want to eat them.

stories.title: Egy évtizede gyűrik, a más uniós országokban dolgozó magyarok jogairól is szól egy új alku
stories.summary: Két bedőlt egyezség után ismét megállapodtak a szociális jogokról szóló javaslatról. Az általában a keleti munkaerőnél felmerülő „szociális dömping” ellen is fellépnének.

stories.title: Hat kategóriadíjat és a nagydíjat is elnyerték a Telex fotósai a Magyar Sajtófotó Pályázaton
stories.summary: Melegh Noémi Napsugár több díj mellett az André Kertész Nagydíjat is elhozta, Bődey Jánost is kétszer díjazták.

stories.title: Ezek voltak 2025 legjobb magyar sajtófotói
stories.summary: Halmozottan sérült, autizmussal élő kisgyerekről készült sorozat és az éneklő Magyar Péterről készült fotó is nyert az idei, sorban a 44. Sajtófotó Pályázaton, ahol a Telex fotósait is bőségesen díjazták.

stories.title: A magyarok 1945 óta törvénytelenül használják a földeket, mondta a szlovák kormány alelnöke
stories.summary: Robert Kaliňák a jövőbeni magyar-szlovák viszony egyik legkényesebb pontjáról, a Beneš-dekrétumokról beszélt.

stories.title: Szabó Bence panaszt nyújtott be az elbocsátása miatt
stories.summary: A Magyar Helsinki Bizottság szerint Szabó Bence jogviszonyát szabályellenesen, anyagi és erkölcsi sérelmet okozva szüntette meg a rendőrség.

stories.title: Újabb minisztereket jelent be pénteken Magyar Péter
stories.summary: Köztük lesz a leendő oktatási miniszter is. Eddig kilenc minisztert jelentett be a hamarosan felálló Tisza-kormányból.

stories.title: Önök milliárdosok? – a Szijjártó-interjú legerősebb pillanatai
stories.summary: Az oroszokkal való „pragmatikus viszony”, KGBéla elengedése, a felesége több százmilliós nyereségű cége, Mészáros Lőrinc, jachtozás Szíjj Lászlóval és a személyes jövője a Fideszben. A Szijjártó Péterrel készült több mint háromórás interjúnk legfontosabb pillanatai.

stories.title: Ukrajna a fronton megállította az orosz gőzhengert, de a napi működéshez égető szüksége van az uniós hitelre
stories.summary: A Barátság olajvezeték megnyitásával kötötte össze a leköszönő Orbán-kormány a 90 milliárd eurós uniós hitelt, amit keddtől nem blokkol tovább. Az utolsó előtti pillanatban érkezhet meg a pénz, hogy ne kelljen áthidaló kölcsönt felvenniük az ukránoknak. A Tisza-kormány új lapot nyithat a magyar–ukrán viszonyban, de maradnak régi kérdések. Rácz Andrással elemeztük az elmúlt időszak történéseit.

stories.title: „Hosszú időre túszul ejtett minket” – búcsúztak az EU-csúcsot kihagyó Orbántól az uniós kollégái
stories.summary: Belgium miniszterelnöke szerint egy kicsit túl nagy az eufória a magyar kormányfő hiánya miatt, és arra utalt, hogy Magyar Péterrel se feltétlenül lesz könnyű mindenben megegyezni.

stories.title: Ukraine says Russian oil to Hungary, Slovakia has restarted
stories.summary: Energy companies in Ukraine and Hungary have both said that oil flows through the Druzhba pipeline from Russia via Belarus have restarted. Hungary and Slovakia expect the first supplies to arrive by Thursday.

stories.title: EU unblocks €90 billion loan to Ukraine
stories.summary: Envoys from EU member states have finally paved the way for a €90 billion loan to Ukraine that had long been blocked by Hungary. They also approved a 20th package of sanctions on Russia over its invasion of its neighbor.

stories.title: Hungary: Orban-era LGBTQ law infringes human rights, ECJ rules
stories.summary: The EU's top court has found that Hungary's 2021 laws on issues including transgender and homosexual identity and sex offenses breach various EU laws. This includes Article 2 of EU Treaty, to protect human dignity.

stories.title: Rat poison found in baby food, extortion suspected
stories.summary: Rat poison has been found in HiPP baby food jars across Austria, Czechia and Slovakia in what authorities suspect is a blackmail attempt.

stories.title: Revealed: UK oil refinery owner moved Russian loans to offshore subsidiary where sanctions did not apply
stories.summary: MPs call for investigation into Essar Energy, owner of Stanlow refinery, which shifted loans from ‘Putin’s piggy bank’ VTB to Mauritius Days after the first wave of Russian tanks surged over the border into Ukraine in March 2022, dockers at a port in northern England took a stand. Appalled by Vladimir Putin’s brutality, workers at Ellesmere Port in Cheshire vowed never to unload any Russian oil destined for the nearby Stanlow refinery, a major hub for UK fuel supplies. stories.title: Présidentielle : qui veut quoi, à gauche ?
stories.summary: Il y a ceux qui avancent en solo mais espèrent un ralliement de leurs partenaires, ceux qui ne jurent que par une primaire, ceux qui n’en veulent pas, mais misent tout de même sur une union des gauches non-mélenchonistes… Bref, c’est à n’y plus rien comprendre. Mais n’allez pas vous faire mal aux cheveux, POLITICO a fait le point pour vous. stories.title: ICC says Philippines' Duterte can stand trial over war on drugs
stories.summary: The ICC has said there is enough evidence linking former Philippines president Rodrigo Duterte to crimes against humanity over his role in orchestrating a deadly anti-drugs crackdown.

stories.title: US soldier allegedly made $400k on Polymarket betting on Maduro raid
stories.summary: Prosecutors say the soldier used classified information of the Maduro operation to place winning bets on Polymarket. The case comes amid other incidents of prediction market bets tied to US government actions.

stories.summary: Forty-four lawmakers could be banned from office for seeking to change Thailand's strict lese-majeste law, which criminalizes criticism of the monarchy. Critics say it has been used to silence opponents.

stories.title: One in 10 operations in England cancelled with less than 24 hours’ notice
stories.summary: Nearly 40% of cancellations could be avoided, researchers say About one in 10 operations in England are cancelled with less than 24 hours’ notice or postponed, according to research.. A study of elective surgery at 91 English NHS trusts found that 10% of operations were cancelled the day before the planned surgery date; while 9% were postponed when patients had their pre-op appointment. stories.summary: Industry sceptical as Nigel Farage’s party goes on charm offensive to help win over former Labour heartlands Reform UK has asked steel bosses to draw up an “alternative steel strategy” to rival recent government plans, stoking industry fears over a charm offensive by Nigel Farage’s party as it eyes gains in former Labour heartlands. stories.title: Philippines’ ex-president Rodrigo Duterte to face trial for crimes against humanity

stories.title: Take action over officials in Kyrgyzstan ‘helping Russia evade sanctions’, MPs and peers say
stories.summary: British foreign secretary told to impose new measures as ruble-pegged cryptocurrency A7A5 is supported in country More than 20 MPs and peers have called on the foreign secretary to take action against institutions and individuals in Kyrgyzstan allegedly facilitating large-scale Russian sanctions evasion. stories.title: ‘Hairdryer or lighter?’: French police look at claim of sensor tampering to win weather bets
stories.summary: Forecasting service raises alarm over data from Paris airport used to settle Polymarket wagers on temperature French police are investigating alleged tampering with national weather forecasting service equipment after a series of unusual temperature readings coincided with suspicious winning bets made on Polymarket. Data from a Météo-France weather station at Paris’s Charles de Gaulle airport was used to settle bets between online gamblers on what the temperature would be in Paris for March and

stories.title: Warner Bros shareholders greenlight sale to Paramount
stories.summary: The deal would combine two major Hollywood studios and bring CBS and CNN under one roof, tightening an already concentrated media landscape, critics say.

stories.title: Trump says Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extended by three weeks
stories.summary: Donald Trump's remarks follow a meeting at the White House with high-ranking officials from Israel and Lebanon. The extension comes after Israel struck southern Lebanon and Hezbollah fired rockets at Israel.

stories.title: Meta, Microsoft purge jobs amid AI build-up

stories.summary: West Bengal and Tamil Nadu recorded the highest-ever voter turnout in state polls after controversial voter roll revision. DW has more.

Il devra donc faire face aux critiques récurrentes, et pas toujours fondées, dont cette technologie fait l’objet, les PAC étant souvent présentées comme coûteuses, bruyantes ou encore inefficaces en cas de températures négatives. Les acteurs de la filière gazière en particulier, qui considèren

stories.title: Kazakhstan confirms suspension of its oil transit to Germany via Russia
stories.summary: Kazakhstan’s energy minister announced Wednesday there would be no transit of its oil to Germany through Russia throughout May due to Russian technical constraints, assuming it is “related to recent strikes on Russian infrastructure".

stories.summary: Bills are seeking to change section that opposition says makes Godwin Friday, a dual citizen, ineligible to be PM The St Vincent and the Grenadines government has delayed a controversial effort to amend a section of the country’s constitution that the opposition says renders the prime minister ineligible for his position in parliament. Two bills, among six listed for the parliament session on Tuesday this week, were aimed at clarifying a section of the 1979 constitution governing the citizenship

stories.title: Prince Harry tells Putin 'there is still a moment to stop this war', at Kyiv conference
stories.summary: Duke of Sussex was speaking at the Kyiv Security Forum during a surprise visit to show support for Ukraine after four years of war with Russia. The UK military also offered to deploy mine-hunting drones and specialist divers to help clear the strait mined by Iran – but no decision has been made on w

stories.title: Trump may talk of regime infighting, but Iran seems united by strategy born of war

stories.title: Bayern Munich on the brink of historic 'double treble'

stories.title: Iran: Daily life shadowed by war, scarcity and fear
stories.summary: The US naval blockade aims to put pressure on the Iranian regime, but ordinary Iranians mostly feel the effects. Many of them find their lives shaped by growing exhaustion.

stories.title: Germany unveils first ever military strategy for Bundeswehr
stories.summary: For the first time in its history, the Bundeswehr has adopted a military strategy to prepare Germany for future threats. Large sections of the document are classified.

stories.title: UK: Health data volunteered by 500,000 people to Biobank charity listed for sale on Alibaba in China

stories.summary: The Pope gives a frank and passionate speech, the likes of which have characterised his tour of Africa.

stories.summary: The US and Iran continued seizing ships in the tense Hormuz standoff, with Tehran calling Washington's blockade a “ceasefire violation” and claiming it has been collecting transit fees for passage.

stories.title: Pope Leo XIV ends Africa tour with open-air mass in Equatorial Guinea

stories.title: US-Kuwaiti journalist held in Kuwait over social media posts acquitted, lawyers say

stories.title: Number of billionaires globally could reach 4,000 in next five years

stories.summary: A rendőr- és tűzoltónapon rendezett ünnepségen olyan állami vezetők is kitüntetést vehettek át, akik nem is ezen a területen dolgoznak.

stories.title: Trump nagyon nyomta, hogy az államok rajzolják át a választókerületeket, de újra és újra visszafelé sül el a terve
stories.summary: Új szintre emelték a gerrymanderinget az USA-ban, a republikánus államok egymás után kezdték el úgy átalakítani a körzeteket, hogy bebiztosítsák saját győzelmüket. A demokraták felvették a kesztyűt, és a héten Virginiában értek el fontos sikert.

stories.title: Iran war: Jet fuel crisis deepens as Lufthansa cuts flights
stories.summary: The impact of jet fuel shortages because of the closure of the Strait of Hormuz is starting to burn. Lufthansa has canceled 20,000 flights and experts warn more cancellations and price hikes are on the way.

stories.title: Could Iran war trigger bigger trade crisis than COVID?
stories.summary: As the Iran war simmers, trade experts ask whether it could reshape supply chains more deeply than COVID-19, forcing companies to rethink strategies to counter further geopolitical shocks.

stories.title: Avoid men, German police association chief tells women
stories.summary: Police association chief Dirk Peglow has stirred debate with his advice to women to avoid relationships with men for safety's sake. But his provocative tip is not without reason, as statistics show.

stories.title: The rise and fall of East Germany's Palace of the Republic
stories.summary: From 1976 through 1990, the building in East Berlin served as a symbol of the communist state's power. The "People's Palace" was controversially destroyed — and yet remains present.

stories.title: Russia to block Kazakh oil flows to Germany via key pipeline
stories.summary: Germany has confirmed that Russia will block flows of Kazakh oil to a vital refinery that supplies much of Berlin's energy needs from May 1.

stories.title: Germany's finance minister wants to scrap spousal tax splitting
stories.summary: Highly controversial, but not well understood: German Finance Minister Lars Klingbeil wants to change the tax benefits for married couples.

stories.title: Germany's Merz: Climate protection must not hold economy back
stories.summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said the public would not accept climate policies that lead to deindustrialization, warning that they would hinder progress and innovation.

stories.title: UK, France agree 3‑year deal to curb Channel crossings
stories.summary: For the first time, the deal links some UK payments to France to performance in stopping migrants. Funding will be halted after one year if targets are not met.

stories.title: Denmark: Two trains collide injuring several
stories.summary: Two trains have crashed into each other in Denmark, leaving several people critically injured, authorities have said.

stories.title: Pope Leo XIV condemns ‘lust for power’ and inequality in Equatorial Guinea
stories.summary: The Holy Father of the Catholic Church made a stop in the final leg of his four-country tour of Africa. Equatorial Guinea is a secular country, with around three-fourths of its population Catholic.

stories.title: Iran's Revolutionary Guards tighten grip on power as civilian leadership sidelined
stories.summary: Iran’s Revolutionary Guard has evolved from a theocratic wartime force into a dominant military-economic power. Over decades, it expanded regionally, reshaped Iran’s internal balance of power, now standing at the centre of a shifting political structure amid leadership changes and consolidation.

stories.title: Fed chair nominee Warsh rejects 'Trump sock puppet' label at Senate hearing
stories.summary: Kevin Warsh, the nominee for Chair of the US Federal Reserve, declared he has not pledged to lower interest rates to secure his position despite public calls from US President Donald Trump for immediate monetary easing.

stories.title: Madrid hosts Experience Macao Roadshow showcasing culture, food and tourism
stories.summary: The Experience Macao Roadshow in Madrid offered visitors interactive activities, cultural performances and gastronomy highlighting the Asian territory’s tourism appeal.

stories.title: Syria's president arrives in Qatar for talks after Saudi Arabia visit
stories.summary: Syria’s president Ahmed al-Sharaa visited Doha on Wednesday for talks with Qatar’s emir on economic cooperation, investment and post-al-Assad regional reintegration.

stories.title: Aerial footage shows widespread destruction in southern Lebanon
stories.summary: A 10-day ceasefire went into effect in Lebanon on Friday, but there have been several Israeli strikes and Hezbollah claimed its first attack on Tuesday.

stories.title: Billionaire backer sues Trump family's crypto firm over alleged extortion
stories.summary: Billionaire investor Justin Sun is suing the family's World Liberty crypto venture after spending $45m on its tokens.

stories.title: Second French peacekeeper dies after ambush blamed on Hezbollah, Macron says
stories.summary: Established in 1978, UNIFIL has more than 10,000 peacekeepers from 50 countries who patrol the Blue Line, the UN-drawn border between Lebanon and Israel.

stories.title: Beijing artist marks World Earth Day with peace installation
stories.summary: Chinese artist Kong Ning marks World Earth Day in Beijing with a peace-themed installation, using wearable art to highlight environmental concerns.

stories.title: Girl, 14, found in singer D4vd's car died of 'multiple injuries'
stories.summary: The medical examiner said the 14-year-old's cause of death was determined months ago but was blocked from release.

stories.title: Donald Trump says the US is in no rush to end war but 'clock is ticking' for Iran
stories.summary: Earlier in the day, Trump said he had ordered the US Navy to "shoot and kill" any boats found laying sea mines in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Student allegedly jailed in China for six years after taking part in pro-democracy protests in Australia
stories.summary: Exclusive: The Australian government has been urged to take stronger action to protect overseas students from political repression The Australian government has been urged to take stronger action to protect Chinese international students from political repression by authorities on their return after a Chinese student was allegedly sentenced to six years’ imprisonment for joining pro-democracy protests in Australia. The student, who the Guardian has chosen not to name, lost contact with his frien

stories.title: Taiwan president blames China for forced cancellation of Eswatini trip
stories.summary: Lai Ching-te abandons visit after Seychelles, Mauritius and Madagascar revoke overflight permission Taiwan’s president, Lai Ching-te, has cancelled his trip to Eswatini, the democratic island’s only diplomatic ally in Africa, after his government said several countries had revoked overflight permits because of “intense pressure” from China. Lai was to leave on Wednesday for the 40th anniversary of King Mswati III’s accession. Continue reading...

stories.title: Millions in India stripped of vote before critical state election, as government seeks to ‘purify’ electoral roll
stories.summary: Experts say Muslims and other minorities have been disproportionately deleted from the electoral roll ahead of the West Bengal elections this week Millions of people in the Indian state of West Bengal have been stripped of their vote ahead of a critical state election this week, after a controversial electoral revision described by critics as a “bloodless political genocide” and mass disenfranchisement of minorities. In West Bengal, a total of 9.1 million names have been deleted from the registe

stories.title: Heatwaves, floods and wildfires pose rising threat to democracy, report finds
stories.summary: Research shows natural hazards linked to climate crisis disrupted 23 elections in 18 countries in 2024 Democracy is under mounting threat from the climate crisis, with new analysis documenting how elections are increasingly shaped not only by political forces but also by floods, wildfires and extreme weather. At least 94 elections and referendums across 52 countries have been disrupted by climate-related impacts over the last two decades, researchers found. Continue reading...
